reasoning: The flagged unsafe files are development/CI scripts not executed during makepkg build/install. PKGBUILD and .install scripts are safe.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged development scripts (Docker/uploader) as security risks despite them not being executed during package build/installation
coverage_issues: deepseek missed Docker and uploader scripts (though irrelevant to build safety)
qwen overlooked critical extension files reviewed by deepseek (base.js, common.js, etc.)
false_positives: close-docker.sh: Development script, not executed during build
upload.py: CI helper, credentials handled locally not during install
run-docker.sh: Docker test environment, not used in packaging
run_and_kill.py: Testing script, absent from build process
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Weight PKGBUILD/.install higher than CI/dev scripts
Enhance context awareness: Models must better distinguish build-time vs development artifacts
Adjust risk assessment: Docker/CI scripts irrelevant unless called by PKGBUILD functions
Optimize file selection: Prevent models from over-indexing on non-build directories like docker/
